meaning: n.杂乱的一团,纠葛,纷扰,误解
examples: Justice and conspiracy dispute. Money and love imbroglio. Strong and weak game.
The British imbroglio was the first public smudge on Costner's family - man image.
Like Andreessen, Ray has, since the Hurd imbroglio, been the public face of the board.
Would culture have prompted HP to be less secretive about the Mark Hurd imbroglio?
